components.name: disodium hydrogen phosphate
components.evidence: standard M9 minimal media (50 mM Na2HPO4, 20 mM KH2PO4, 1 mM NaCl, 20 mM NH4Cl, 2 mM MgSO4 and 100 μM CaCl2, 134 μM EDTA, 13 μM FeCl3·6H2O, 6.2 μM ZnCl2, 0.76 μM CuCl2·2H2O, 0.42 μM CoCl2·2H2O, 1.62 μM H3BO3, 0.081 μM MnCl2·4H2O)

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: we supplied the NNmini strain growing on 10 mM glucose with different concentrations of acetate, pyruvate or casamino acids

components.name: pyruvate
components.evidence: max. OD = 0.47 with 8 mM pyruvate, growth rate = 0.108 ± 0.003 h−1

components.name: oxygen
components.evidence: Growth experiments were carried out ... in standard M9 minimal media ... (aerobic shaking cultures)

media.base_medium: standard M9 minimal medium (fully specified in main text)

sources_chased.what: paper main text 'Media and growth experiments' section
added: explicit concentrations for all 13 salts/trace metals + glucose + pyruvate

media.notes: The paper (Nat Commun 2024, doi:10.1038/s41467-024-51029-x) fully defines this M9 medium inline in the main text, including all trace metals (Fe, Zn, Cu, Co, B, Mn), EDTA chelator, and the CaCl2. No cited base-medium reference or supplement was required. Recipe was already complete in the target's verification_evidence; this run confirms it and assigns exact concentrations. EDTA and boric acid have no standalone BiGG exchange (they appear in the target's current_uncovered) but are genuine ingredients of the medium and are retained here. Carbon: 10 mM glucose + 8 mM pyruvate supplement; aerobic.
